name: AETHER_02
description: Full-spectrum Linux MCP server — 12 tools, 99.9% system management coverage including GUI automation, hardware control, security audit, package management. Separate repo from AETHER (Windows).
author: foursecondfivefour

tools.name: process_control
tools.description: Linux process management — list, kill, signal, create, priority, threads, CPU affinity, memory limits, file descriptors, environment, cgroups v2, namespaces, OOM, seccomp, ptrace, Docker/Podman containers, perf

tools.name: file_system
tools.description: Linux filesystem operations — read/write/delete, stat, ACL, xattr, symlinks, chmod/chown, mount/umount/remount, disks, partitions, mdraid, LVM, swap, fsck, NFS, btrfs, ZFS, overlay

tools.name: package_manager
tools.description: Universal package management — apt, dnf, pacman, zypper, flatpak, snap, AppImage, nix. Install, remove, upgrade, search, hold, repos, history, verify

tools.name: system_config
tools.description: Linux system configuration — sysctl, kernel cmdline, modprobe, dracut, dconf, gsettings, limits.conf, hostname, timezone, locale, keyboard, /etc configs, udev rules, environment, os-release

tools.name: service_manager
tools.description: Service management — systemd (start/stop/restart/enable/disable/mask/reload/isolate/timers/sockets/mounts/analyze), OpenRC (rc-service/rc-update), runit (sv), s6

tools.name: gui_automation
tools.description: GUI automation — X11 (XTest/XInput2) + Wayland (ydotool/wlrctl), mouse/keyboard, window management, screenshots, clipboard, display (xrandr), audio (PulseAudio/PipeWire), brightness, night light, notifications, screensaver

tools.name: system_info
tools.description: Linux system information — CPU, memory, disk, OS release, kernel, modules, uptime, load, GPU, PCI, USB, DMI/SMBIOS, ACPI, EDID, IOMMU, NUMA, IRQ, lm-sensors, devices, drivers, firmware

tools.name: network_manager
tools.description: Linux network management — iproute2 (link/addr/route), nftables firewall, NetworkManager (DBus), WireGuard, bridges/bonds/VLANs, network namespaces, tc/qdisc, socket diag, WiFi, Bluetooth, DNS (systemd-resolved), proxy, hosts

tools.name: user_management
tools.description: Linux user administration — /etc/passwd/group/shadow, useradd/userdel/usermod, groupadd/groupdel, sessions (loginctl), nsswitch.conf, PAM, polkit, sudoers, SSH authorized_keys, faillock, subuid/subgid

tools.name: security_audit
tools.description: Linux security auditing — SELinux, AppArmor, auditd, ufw/firewalld, TPM2, Secure Boot, IMA/EVM, fapolicyd, kernel lockdown, LSMs, spectre/meltdown mitigations, ASLR/NX/RELRO, file integrity (AIDE), UEFI boot chain

tools.name: hardware_control
tools.description: Linux hardware control — NVIDIA/AMD/Intel GPU, PCI rescan, USB authorization, ACPI, cpufreq governors, thermal zones/sensors, CPU microcode, IOMMU groups, IRQ affinity, HugePages, kdump

tools.name: system_automation
tools.description: Linux system automation — journald query/follow/vacuum, systemd timers, cron/anacron, at jobs, tmpfiles.d, sysusers.d, binfmt_misc, modules-load.d, sysctl.d, boot analysis (systemd-analyze)

prompts.name: system_health
prompts.description: Analyze CPU, memory, disk, OS, and process health. Check load, uptime, swap usage, disk space, and top processes with optional service status.

prompts.name: security_posture
prompts.description: Audit Linux security posture — SELinux/AppArmor status, firewall rules, kernel hardening, ASLR, Secure Boot, TPM, file integrity. Generate a risk-scored security report.

prompts.name: diagnostic_report
prompts.description: Generate a structured diagnostic report — journal errors, failing services, disk health, OOM events, network issues, and recent crashes. Include actionable remediation steps.

resources.name: cpu_info
resources.description: Live CPU metrics — model, cores, frequency, governor, vulnerabilities, cache

resources.name: memory_info
resources.description: Live memory metrics — total, used, available, buffer/cache, swap, hugepages

resources.name: disk_info
resources.description: Disk layout — lsblk output with partitions, filesystems, mount points

resources.name: os_release
resources.description: OS identification — distribution, version, kernel, architecture

resources.name: uptime_info
resources.description: System uptime, load averages, and boot time

resources.name: kernel_info
resources.description: Kernel version, modules loaded, tainted state

resources.name: gpu_info
resources.description: GPU list from lspci — vendor, model, driver

resources.name: pci_devices
resources.description: Full PCI bus enumeration with device details

resources.name: usb_devices
resources.description: USB device tree with vendor/product IDs

resources.name: dmi_system
resources.description: DMI/SMBIOS system information — manufacturer, product, serial

resources.name: thermal_zones
resources.description: Thermal zone temperatures from /sys/class/thermal and sensors

resources.name: network_adapters
resources.description: Network interfaces — IP addresses, MAC, speed, driver, statistics

resources.name: firewall_status
resources.description: Firewall state — ufw/firewalld/nftables rules overview

resources.name: services_status
resources.description: Systemd service status — active, enabled, failed, masked units

resources.name: users_groups
resources.description: Local users and groups from /etc/passwd and /etc/group

resources.name: security_status
resources.description: Security subsystem status — SELinux/AppArmor, auditd, Secure Boot, kernel lockdown

resources.name: feature_gates
resources.description: Current feature gate configuration from .env

resources.name: environment
resources.description: System environment variables

resources.name: installed_packages
resources.description: List of installed packages via detected package manager

resources.name: journal_recent
resources.description: Recent journal entries since last boot
